meta.title: Site Health & Safety Induction Pack
meta.subtitle: Site-specific induction, verification record & refresher schedule — {{site}}
meta.author: HSE Operations
meta.deid_notice: Inductee names appear ONLY on the signed competence-verification record (a legitimate signed record). Any widely distributed copy of this pack uses role labels; no inductee health/medical detail appears anywhere in this pack, and no re-identification key is part of this document.

sections.text: This induction pack is for {{audience: permanent new starters | contractors | visitors | agency/temporary}} at {{named site/project/asset}} ({{sector}}). It is built over the mandatory induction baseline (KB-SNIP-INDUCTION-BASELINE) — emergency, welfare, site rules, site-specific hazards, incident reporting — with this named site's real arrangements layered on; a generic induction is not produced. Delivery: {{face-to-face | e-learning | blended}}. Legal-induction baseline: {{UK MHSWR reg. 10/13 | US OSHA orientation | India Factories Act s.111A → hse-india}}. ISO 45001:2018 cl. 7.2 (competence) & 7.3 (awareness). Decision-support only; a competent person must review this output.

Every topic below is grounded in THIS named site's real arrangement — not generic boilerplate. The five baseline topics (emergency · welfare & first aid · site rules · site-specific hazards & controls · incident/concern reporting) are each layered with the site's specifics; a baseline topic with no named site arrangement is flagged [GAP], never filled with boilerplate.

items: Emergency arrangements — {{named muster point(s) + evacuation routes for THIS site}}
Welfare & first aid — {{first-aiders, first-aid location, welfare facilities for THIS site}}
Site rules — {{access/egress, PPE rules, traffic management, PTW awareness for THIS site}}
Site-specific hazards & controls — {{the real hazards of THIS site, controls ranked via KB-SNIP-HOC}}
Incident & concern reporting — {{how to report a hazard/near-miss + the no-blame expectation}}

rows.control: {{Eliminate/segregate the named site hazard at source — e.g. pedestrian/FLT segregation}}
{{Engineering control for the named hazard — e.g. mezzanine edge protection}}
{{Administrative control — e.g. one-way traffic routes + signage}}
{{PPE — e.g. hi-vis in the yard (only where higher-order controls justified)}}

items: Alarm & evacuation — {{the alarm signal, the named evacuation routes}}
Muster point(s) — {{the actual named muster point for THIS site}}
First aid — {{named first-aiders, first-aid room/kit location}}
On discovering an incident — {{the site's first-response instruction (ties to emergency-response-plan #15)}}

sections.caption: Competence-verification record — per inductee (role-labelled in this shared copy; named inductees only on the signed original); level set on KB-DATA-COMPETENCE-LEVELS by role task-risk. An induction with no verification record fails the quality gate.

rows: Routine + on change of site rules
sections.caption: Refresher schedule — SMART (named owner + ISO due date); higher-risk roles refresh more often. No anonymous actions, no ASAP.

sections.text: Inductee names belong ONLY on the signed competence-verification original. This circulated pack uses role labels; no inductee health/medical detail appears here, and no re-identification key is embedded.

Prepared by {{induction owner role}}; SME review (persona: Site HSE Manager) ran before output — no generic-only content, every topic tied to a named site arrangement, the verification record present, no inductee health detail in this shared copy. This induction pack is decision-support only — it precedes and does NOT replace the human competent-person sign-off, and it does not certify any individual as inducted. Basis: ISO 45001:2018 cl. 7.2/7.3 + the resolved jurisdiction's legal-induction baseline + KB-SNIP-INDUCTION-BASELINE + KB-DATA-COMPETENCE-LEVELS.

items: Baseline: KB-SNIP-INDUCTION-BASELINE (emergency · welfare · rules · site hazards · reporting)
Verification scale: KB-DATA-COMPETENCE-LEVELS (aware → trained → competent → expert)
Related siblings via KB-SNIP-OPS-CLAUSE-MAP (competence-matrix → training-needs-analysis #13; emergency plan → emergency-response-plan #15; contractor vetting → contractor-prequalification #16)
